items.title: Direct Google Business Profile integration (OAuth) — owner-authenticated read + write actions
items.notes: FOLLOW-ON to P7 (seo-engine-p7-gbp-reviews-local-layer), which benchmarks PUBLIC GBP data via DataForSEO business_listings_search (rating, review count, public completeness signals — no auth, read-only, advisory recs). This item is the AUTHENTICATED connection via the Google Business Profile API (My Business Account Management + Business Information + Reviews + Performance APIs): (a) READ owner-only metrics not in public data — profile views/searches, call/direction/website/booking action counts, query discovery; and (b) WRITE — reply to reviews, publish GBP posts, edit hours/attributes/categories/photos, all from inside the platform, turning P7's advisory review-gap/GBP-completeness recs into one-click executable actions. Requires: a Google Cloud OAuth client + GBP API access APPROVAL (Google gates this behind an application/allowlist — non-trivial lead time and the biggest risk), a per-location encrypted OAuth token store (mirror the Stripe AES-256-GCM config-on-disk pattern), a 'Connect Google Business Profile' flow in the locations editor (server/local-seo locations CRUD already stores gbp_place_id), token refresh, and activity/audit logging for write actions. Distinct from intel-quality-google-business-profile-health (read-only PUBLIC health signals). Until GBP API access is approved, P7's public benchmarking stands as the read-only product.

items.title: Per-market keyword relevance — keyword↔market assignment model
items.notes: Today the refresh planner pairs every selected keyword with every active market — `dentist at houston` gets queried against Austin's market and surfaces Houston competitors as 'top local results' in the Austin context. Confirmed on Swish staging refresh 2026-05-21 (55 of 200 snapshots = 27.5% cross-market noise). Fix is non-trivial because it requires a keyword↔market assignment model. (1) New `keyword_markets` join table mapping which keywords should be checked against which markets. (2) Default: keyword applies to all active markets (matches current implicit behavior, no migration of existing data needed). (3) Override path: admin pins a keyword to specific market(s). (4) Auto-inference: if keyword text contains an active market's city/region name, default assignment to that market only. (5) Iterator + refresh planner respect the assignment. (6) UI: per-market detail blocks in keyword drawer instead of single block, with pin/unpin controls. (7) **Per-market volume/clicks/impressions reporting**: the keyword detail surface should show separate volume, GSC clicks, GSC impressions, and CTR per active market the keyword applies to — today these are workspace-global and don't distinguish between Austin and Houston performance for the same keyword. Requires per-market metric storage AND geo-targeted DataForSEO volume queries (see intel-quality-keyword-volume-geo-targeting). Deferred to P2 because no current client uses multi-market in production — Swish is the only multi-market workspace and is internal. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but re-confirm priority. The item is still framed correctly for the current architecture (the planner pairing it describes is verbatim what exists). The deferral rationale also still holds: per the notes, Swish is the only multi-market workspace and it's internal — so the 27.5% cross-market noise has zero production client impact today. Recommend keeping P2/deferred and gating activation on the FIRST paying multi-market client rather than building speculatively. When built, sub-points 1-5 (join table, default-all, pin override, city-name auto-inference, planner respect) are the load-bearing core; sub-points 6-7 (per-market UI + per-market metrics) depend on the also-pending geo-targeted volume item and should be a follow-up phase.

Scope may include edit-session lifecycle, staged deltas, and optional server AI wrapper endpoints while preserving existing service-layer writes, activity provenance (`source: mcp-chat`), and broadcast invalidation contracts. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · OWNER DECISION · high conf]: Escalate to owner. This is a deliberate conditional defer, not a missing capability. The v1 conflict/version/provenance safety net already covers the named risks, so there is no code-determinable trigger to build it. Keep the item parked as 'deferred — revisit only on observed operational pain'; do not promote to a build queue without evidence of high MCP edit conflict rates or failed-apply complaints.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — Schema Intelligence & Authority
sprints.rationale: Highest strategic SEO differentiation and trust-evidence value. Prioritizes schema intelligence upgrades that improve rich-result eligibility, E-E-A-T signaling, and entity grounding.

items.title: Schema Page-Element Catalog PR3 — polish (codeBlocks, headings/ToC, speakable)
items.notes: Optional polish: codeBlocks → CodeRepository/SoftwareSourceCode (low ROI for agency sites); headings → WebPage.speakable cssSelector (overlaps with schema-engagement-signals; whichever ships first wins). May be skipped if cycles are short. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but LOW priority, and re-scope to drop the codeBlocks half. The spec itself rates codeBlocks→SoftwareSourceCode as 'low ROI for agency sites' and the roadmap note agrees. The only piece with real SEO value is headings→WebPage.speakable cssSelector, which fully overlaps schema-engagement-signals (per the spec's own §5.3/§ note: 'whichever lands first wins'). Recommendation: fold the speakable cssSelector deliverable into schema-engagement-signals and mark this item obsolete-once-that-ships, or keep it as a thin 'headings extraction + speakable' task. The codeBlocks portion should be explicitly dropped/deferred indefinitely.

items.title: Schema engagement signals — wordCount, timeRequired, speakable, educationalLevel
items.notes: Article.wordCount shipped in the Blog/Article Schema Quality Hardening pass (May 2026) using visible content extraction instead of full articleBody duplication. Remaining quick-win Tier-4 fields with data the platform already has: Article.timeRequired = wordCount/250wpm rounded; WebPage.speakable with cssSelector pointing to .lead/.intro/first-paragraph — voice-assistant rich result; Article.educationalLevel via AI classification (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced). All wire through existing PageData typed boundary. No new admin UI required for the basics; speakable cssSelector is a per-template constant. Independent rich-result eligibility unlock for Google Article + voice-search amplification. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, re-scoped: remove wordCount from the title (it's shipped) and re-frame as the three remaining Tier-4 quick-wins. timeRequired = wordCount/250 is trivial since wordCount already exists. educationalLevel needs a small AI classification step. speakable cssSelector is a per-template constant — and is the SAME deliverable claimed by schema-page-element-catalog-pr3, so consolidate the two: make this item the single owner of speakable and close the PR3 speakable scope here. Net: a ~1-2 day task, not 2 days of net-new for all four.

items.title: Schema E-E-A-T amplifiers — publishingPrinciples, reviewedBy, copyrightHolder, isAccessibleForFree, Person.jobTitle
items.notes: Tier-3 trust signals Google increasingly weighs. Add 4 new optional Workspace fields: publishingPrinciplesUrl, actionableFeedbackPolicyUrl (both → Organization.publishingPrinciples + actionableFeedbackPolicy on every page's @graph). Article.copyrightHolder ref to Organization, copyrightYear from datePublished. Article.reviewedBy as Person from new CMS field 'fact_checker' (optional). Article.isAccessibleForFree from per-tier content gating. Person.jobTitle + worksFor on author when CMS author records have those fields (currently they're just string names). Mostly admin Settings UI work + template emission. Each field is an additive E-E-A-T signal — non-breaking, immediate ranking benefit on YMYL content. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, re-scoped: drop Person.jobTitle from the title (shipped, with worksFor + hasCredential as a bonus). The remaining real work is the four org/article trust-policy fields: publishingPrinciplesUrl + actionableFeedbackPolicyUrl (new Workspace Settings fields → Organization node), Article.copyrightHolder/copyrightYear (derivable from existing org ref + datePublished — near-free), Article.reviewedBy (needs a new optional CMS 'fact_checker' field), Article.isAccessibleForFree (wire from existing per-tier content gating). Mostly admin Settings UI + template emission, lower effort than the original 1w estimate.

items.title: Schema commerce types — Service.offers (PriceSpecification), Product, Course, Event
items.notes: Tier-5 monetization opportunity for agencies with productized services / paid content. Service.offers with Offer { price, priceCurrency, availability } — Google's 'service prices' SERP feature. Product schema for templates/digital products. Course for educational content/training. Event for webinars/launches with eventStatus + eventAttendanceMode (Online/Offline/Mixed). Each is independent rich-result eligibility unlock. Workspace.contentPricing already exists (used for content-purchase tiers); Service.offers can reuse the pricing infra. Per-type effort similar to parity-fields PR1's per-template additions; parallelizable across template files. Default-off; admin opts in per-workspace. Most useful for agencies with packaged services (web-design + brand audit + content retainer); skip for service-only sites without published prices. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, re-scoped to just Course + Event (+ optional PriceSpecification on offers). Drop Service.offers and Product from the title — both ship today. Course and Event are genuinely unbuilt and remain valid independent rich-result unlocks, but they're P2 and only matter for agencies with training/webinar content; the deliberate WebPage fallback (not an accidental gap) means this is a real net-new template build, parallelizable per the original note. Confirm demand before building — this may be needs-owner-decision on whether any current clients have Course/Event pages.

items.title: Schema trust/authority — Organization.award, member, hasCredential, numberOfEmployees
items.notes: Tier-6 reputation graph fields. New Workspace.organizationAwards (string[] of Award names → Award entity[]), Workspace.organizationMemberships (industry orgs → ProgramMembership/Organization references), Workspace.organizationCredentials (certifications → EducationalOccupationalCredential), Workspace.numberOfEmployees (QuantitativeValue { minValue, maxValue, unitText: 'employee' }). Each emitted on Organization sibling node in homepage + local-business templates. Pure admin Settings UI + template extension; no AI/intelligence dependencies. Agency credibility in SERP, especially for B2B competitive queries. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P2. This is pure admin-Settings-UI + template-extension work with no AI/intelligence dependency (as the note says). numberOfEmployees is a near-free quick-win since the data field already exists — just add a QuantitativeValue emission to homepage.ts/local-business.ts. award/member/credential need 3 new Workspace string[] fields + Settings UI. Frame correctly as 'low-effort additive Organization fields'; the original 2-3d estimate is realistic given numberOfEmployees is half-done at the data layer.

items.title: Schema Intelligence Layer (FAQ-from-GSC, E-E-A-T, healthcare subtypes)
items.notes: Follow-up to lean-schema-rewrite. Re-injects the platform's intelligence layer surgically into schema generation after the current deterministic schema path is stable: (1) Healthcare type escalation through canonical seoContext/business profile signals, falling back to LocalBusiness only when evidence supports it. (2) FAQ enrichment from GSC question queries via a new slice-backed helper if this feature is revived; the old unused extractFaqOpportunities/schema prompt block was removed in Workspace Intelligence Consolidation PR4. (3) E-E-A-T author injection from content brief evidence into Article.author with Person.knowsAbout/sameAs. (4) Service serviceType from voice profile + business profile. Any revived intelligence enrichment must flow through buildSchemaIntelligence() or another documented central intelligence builder, not direct raw workspace reads. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · medium conf]: Keep as still-relevant but significantly re-scope down. Two of four sub-features are effectively shipped (evidence-based healthcare escalation via semantics.businessType + industrySubtype; E-E-A-T author injection via the eeat-assets slice and article.ts). The remaining genuine work is narrow: (2) FAQ enrichment from GSC question queries (the only sizable piece — needs a new slice-backed helper since the old path was deleted) and (4) wiring serviceType from voice/business profile. Re-frame the item around just those two, flowing through the already-built buildSchemaIntelligence() facade. Drop the original 2-3w estimate to ~1w.

items.title: Schema Pillar 3 — Compile-time + CI Gates
items.notes: DEFERRED. Pillar 3 is regression-prevention hardening (schema-dts compile-time types + schemarama CI corpus + pr-check rule for cross-ref helper imports). It does not change schema output — it locks the bar Pillars 1 + 2 set. Decision (2026-04-29): defer until after schema-yoast-parity-fields ships, because the field expansion will reshape templates and Pillar 3's typed signatures would have to be rewritten. Reactivate after intelligence layer settles. Plan file remains committed for reference.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — Team, Permissions, and Collaboration Controls
sprints.rationale: Unlocks multi-user adoption and safer account expansion by closing core team-management, role-gating, and collaboration workflow gaps.

items.title: Internal team management
items.notes:
Invite team members, manage roles, assign workspaces.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Internal-user backend (roles, invite, workspace assignment) fully shipped via /api/users; admin frontend UI to invite/manage internal team members is not built. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but re-scope to FRONTEND-ONLY: 'Build admin UI to invite/manage internal team members (list, invite, assign workspaces, set owner/admin/member role, deactivate)' wired to the existing /api/users endpoints. Backend is done. Estimate is now ~2-3h (UI + React Query hook + a new admin 'Team' page or Settings tab), not 4-5h. Low risk, P2 still fair given solo-founder (you are currently the only internal user).

items.title: Permission-based feature access
items.notes:
Fine-grained: client_member view-only on approvals, member can't delete workspaces.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Role enum (owner/admin/member, client_owner/client_member) and requireRole middleware shipped; per-action gating (client_member view-only on approvals, member-can't-delete-workspace) is not implemented in routes.
[Platform health recommendation pass 2026-05-26: promoted due to partial migration risk (roles exist, per-action route enforcement still incomplete).] || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant. Re-scope to 'per-action authorization enforcement': add a client-role check in the client-portal auth path so client_member is view-only on approval/decision mutations, and (if internal members ever exist) gate workspace-destructive routes on internal role. Note the architectural subtlety in the re-scope: admin routes use HMAC not JWT, so 'member can't delete workspaces' only matters once internal members log in via JWT — verify that flow exists first. P1 is defensible as a security-hardening item, but real exposure today is low (you control all seats).

items.title: Writer assignment
items.notes:
Assign content briefs to specific writers with notifications.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Adjacent infrastructure exists on work_orders (assigned_to column + mapper); content briefs lack any writer-assignment field and no notification flow is wired. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant. Re-scope precisely: 'Add a writer-assignment field to content briefs (or reuse work_orders.assigned_to), a UI to set it, and an email/in-app notification to the assignee.' Note this only pays off if you have/plan to have external writers — for a solo founder with AI-generated briefs it is low value today, so P2 is generous; consider needs-owner-decision on whether a human-writer workflow is even on the roadmap. If yes, ~2-3h is realistic since work_orders already has the column + mapper to model from.

items.title: Client team collaboration
items.notes: Comments on strategy items, @mentions on briefs, shared notes. Per-seat pricing opportunity. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but re-frame: the generic 'collaboration' need is being incrementally met by the inbox conversation threads, so DON'T build a parallel comment system — extend the existing thread/note model to strategy items and briefs rather than introducing @mentions/shared-notes as a separate feature. This is the largest item (8-10h, P3) and the 'per-seat pricing opportunity' makes it a needs-owner-decision on monetization strategy. Recommend deferring until client base justifies multi-seat collaboration; it duplicates item 32's 'shared notes'. || [DEDUPE 2026-06-24]: the shared-notes sub-scope is owned by item 32 (Shared notes/wiki per workspace) — extend the existing work-order conversation threads to strategy items + briefs; do NOT build a parallel notes/comment system. Per-seat pricing is an owner decision.

items.title: Notification preferences
items.notes:
Per-user email settings, digest frequency.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] In-app notification feed shipped (NotificationBell + useNotifications); per-user email/digest frequency preferences are not built. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant. Re-scope to 'per-user email notification preferences (opt-out per event-type + digest frequency)', building on the existing CLIENT_NOTIFICATION_RECIPIENT_POLICIES table in notification-recipients.ts (the codebase already anticipates this as the branch point). Add a per-user prefs column/table and a settings UI. P3 is fair — low value until you have multiple seats per workspace generating notification noise. ~2-3h estimate is reasonable given the foundation.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — UX Adoption & Onboarding Momentum
sprints.rationale: Improves activation and stickiness through customizable workspace surfaces, guided education, accessibility UX uplift, and onboarding automation.

items.title: Widget System — Customizable Workspace Home dashboard
items.notes: Let users pin/unpin modules on Workspace Home, rearrange widget order, choose sizes (compact/full/wide). Save per-workspace layouts to localStorage or workspace config. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but genuinely optional P2/P3 polish. Framing still accurate. This is admin-only ergonomics (the founder is the only admin), so ROI is modest vs the client-facing items in this batch — consider deprioritizing rather than building soon.

items.title: AI Insights Feed — TikTok-style vertical feed of actionable insights
items.notes:
Swipeable insight cards: 'LCP improved 23%', '3 pages need schema', 'Competitor published 5 articles'. Auto-generated from anomaly detection + audit + strategy data.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] Filterable insight feed UI shipped (src/components/insights/InsightFeed + InsightFeedItem + filter chips); swipeable vertical-feed presentation not built. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep the underlying need (surface actionable insights) but DROP the 'TikTok-style swipeable' presentation framing — poor fit for a B2B SEO analytics tool, and the practical value (filterable, prioritized insight list) is already delivered. Re-scope to 'extend insight feed coverage / client-facing variant' if still wanted, or close the swipeable-UI portion as obsolete. Low priority (P3).

items.title: Command Palette 2.0 — Action palette with natural language
items.notes:
Expand from navigation to actions: 'Generate schema for [page]', 'Create brief for [keyword]', 'Run audit'. Natural language: 'Show thin content pages'.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] CommandPalette.tsx scaffolds an "action" item type alongside nav/workspace/recent, but no natural-language action handlers are wired (no LLM call, no parameterised "Generate schema for [page]"). || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep but re-scope to the precise unbuilt delta: NL parsing + parameterized actions. The executable-action layer (run audit, scan anomalies as real jobs) already exists — update the item to acknowledge that and target only the NL/param layer. Low priority (P3) for a single-admin tool; the deterministic action list already covers common verbs.

items.title: Client Education Hub — Embedded SEO education throughout
items.notes:
Contextual tooltips with 'Why this matters'. 'Learn more' links to SeoGlossary. Video explanations for complex features. Education tip cards in empty states.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] SeoGlossary.tsx + SeoEducationTip.tsx (tab-keyed learnMore content for overview/performance/health/strategy/content/roi) ship; "Why this matters" framing, video embeds, and Glossary cross-links from tips are not wired. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, low priority (P3). Re-scope to the unbuilt delta: (1) reframe tip copy as 'Why this matters', (2) add Glossary cross-links from tips, (3) optionally empty-state education cards. DROP the 'video explanations' sub-scope unless the founder actually wants to produce video — heaviest, lowest-ROI part. Solid client-trust value but secondary to the diagnostics/attention items in this batch.

items.title: Performance Budget Dashboard — Visual page weight tracker
items.notes:
Show page weight over time per workspace. Alert on regressions. Recommendations: 'Homepage grew 400KB, consider image optimization'.
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] PageWeight + PageSpeedPanel + server/performance-store ship point-in-time data; historical weight trend per workspace + regression alerts are not built. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant for the unbuilt scope (historical trend + regression alerting), framed as partially-done. Re-scope/flag a prerequisite the note misses: the storage layer must change first — the current upsert-by-(sub,site_id) destroys history, so building a trend requires either keying snapshots by timestamp or adding a separate weight-history table. Low priority (P3) absent a client asking for weight trends.

items.title: Accessibility Improvements — WCAG AA compliance pass
items.notes:
Add ARIA landmarks, focus management for modals, keyboard navigation for complex widgets. Skip links. Test with screen reader (NVDA/VoiceOver).
[Audit 2026-04-22 partial] 63 aria-label usages across 20 components ship; ARIA landmark roles, skip-links, and modal focus-management are not implemented. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: This is the most accurately-scoped of the three a11y items — make it the canonical WCAG-AA epic and fold 43 + 44 into it (all three are the same work). Re-scope the residual: (1) add ARIA landmarks to the app shell, (2) add a skip link in App.tsx, (3) screen-reader smoke test. The modal-focus line item can be checked off. P2 priority is defensible if pursuing accessibility-conscious clients or compliance positioning. || [MERGE 2026-06-24]: canonical WCAG-AA epic — absorbed item 43 (Accessibility audit expansion: WCAG contrast/ARIA/heading order/form labels) + item 44 (WCAG AA full compliance: focus indicators, keyboard nav). Residual across all three = skip links, ARIA landmark roles, automated axe/heading/contrast check, SR smoke test; modal focus already ships.

items.title: Responsive mobile layout
items.notes: Sidebar → bottom nav, stacked cards on small screens. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant. Re-scope: the highest-value half is the CLIENT dashboard (clients are the ones likely on mobile), not the admin panel (founder works on desktop). Recommend splitting — prioritize client-portal responsive/bottom-nav, treat admin sidebar collapse as P4/optional. The original 'sidebar -> bottom nav, stacked cards' framing is still correct for the client surface.

items.title: Workspace onboarding automation — templates, batch setup, reduce repetitive per-workspace tasks
items.notes: Each new workspace requires: connect Webflow, connect GSC, connect GA4, run keyword analysis, run site audit, set up brand voice, configure competitors, set content pricing, enable client portal. Most steps are identical across workspaces. Add: workspace templates (clone config from existing workspace), batch analysis trigger (run audit + keyword analysis + decay analysis in one click), onboarding checklist with progress tracking, default competitor auto-discovery. Separate initiative from intelligence engine — scoped as its own spec. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, re-scoped to the unbuilt remainder: clone-from-existing-workspace config and a one-click batch-analysis trigger are the two high-leverage pieces for a growing client base (the repetitive-setup pain the item targets). Drop the onboarding-checklist sub-bullet from scope (done). Re-frame the title to 'Workspace cloning + batch setup trigger' to reflect that the checklist half already shipped.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — Operational Automation & Quality Signals
sprints.rationale: Reduces manual operating overhead and increases signal quality with workflow automation, accessibility quality expansion, and strategy-memory follow-through.

items.title: Webhook / Zapier triggers
items.notes: Fire webhooks on key events. Enables automation integrations. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but re-scope and re-justify. The internal WS event taxonomy (server/ws-events.ts) is the natural source for outbound webhook fan-out, so the build is 'subscribe a user-supplied URL to existing workspace events' rather than greenfield event plumbing. Note for owner: the MCP action server may already cover the deeper 'enable automation' intent for AI-driven workflows, so this item is now narrowly about no-code (Zapier/Make) outbound triggers. Low priority (P3) is appropriate; validate demand before building.

items.title: Shared notes / wiki per workspace
items.notes: Team-internal notes about a client. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant and correctly framed, but DEDUPE against item 117 (which also lists 'shared notes') — fold the two shared-notes asks into one. Minimal viable build: a single workspace.internalNotes TEXT column + a notes panel in WorkspaceSettings (admin-only, never serialized to public-portal.ts). P3/2-3h is accurate. Genuinely useful even for a solo founder (durable per-client context/reminders), so this is a cheap, sensible keep — arguably the best ROI item in this theme. || [DEDUPE 2026-06-24]: owns the shared-notes capability that item 117's collaboration sub-scope also referenced — single source of truth. Minimal workspace.internalNotes TEXT + admin panel, never public-serialized.

items.title: Client NPS survey
items.notes: Periodic in-dashboard satisfaction survey. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep — still-relevant, valid, and small. Re-scope to the current architecture: build it as a periodic in-dashboard survey component using existing primitives (SectionCard + a rating control), persist responses to a new typed table, and — importantly per the codebase's intelligence-slice rule — wire the resulting satisfaction signal into ClientSignalsSlice (server/intelligence/) so AdminChat/intelligence can see client sentiment (today it is blind to satisfaction). Low priority (P3) is appropriate given Premium/client base is still small; sequence it after there are enough active client users for NPS to be meaningful.

items.title: Schema auto-schedule
items.notes: Re-generate schemas on cadence, flag stale pages. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P3, small. The original 2-3h estimate is plausible because the hard parts (publish dates, regen path, queue infra) already exist. Two concrete deliverables: (a) add a stale computation to toAdminSchemaPageSuggestionView comparing lastPublishedAt against the schema snapshot's generatedAt to flag stale pages in the existing Schema UI; (b) add a lightweight scheduled regeneration trigger following the established recommendation-regen-scheduler.ts pattern. No new architecture needed — frame as 'wire existing publish-date + regen primitives into a cadence + stale badge.'

items.title: Historical redirect comparison
items.notes: Track redirect status over time, detect new 404s. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant; framing is correct. Note the storage is INSERT OR REPLACE on a timestamped id so prior scans are not reliably retained — a 'detect new 404s vs last scan' feature needs both (a) guaranteed retention of the previous snapshot and (b) a diff computation, neither of which exists. Small (2-3h) but genuinely unbuilt; reasonable to keep at P3 or fold into a broader 'site health over time' effort.

items.title: AI strategy memory & learning
items.notes: AI learns from conversations, approved changes, rejected suggestions. Proprietary knowledge asset. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep but RE-SCOPE heavily and lower the estimate — the bulk (the outcome-based learning loop) is done and shipping. Re-frame the remaining item as two small, distinct additions: (a) capture rejected/dismissed recommendations as a negative learning signal (cheap — a dismiss already fires; record it as a 'declined' datapoint the learnings engine can weigh down that actionType for that workspace), and (b) optionally mine AdminChat conversations for durable preferences/constraints (higher effort, needs a conversation store). The '8-12h / proprietary knowledge asset' framing is stale — most of the asset already exists.

sprints.name: Platform Value Wave — Integrations, Distribution, and Expansion Bets
sprints.rationale: Packages higher-latency integration and expansion work into a distinct growth track so near-term value sprints stay focused while long-horizon platform expansion remains actionable.

items.title: GSC: URL Inspection API
items.notes: Per-URL indexing status, crawl info. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant for the indexing/crawl half. Re-scope to 'extend the existing inspectUrl call to parse indexStatusResult (coverageState, crawledAs, lastCrawlTime, pageFetchState, indexingState) and surface per-URL index status', which is cheaper than the original estimate since auth + the API call + error handling already exist. Flag the dead inspectUrlForRichResults function to the owner as a separate cleanup question (built but unwired). || [CLEANUP 2026-06-24]: also surface inspectUrlForRichResults (search-console.ts) — built but has ZERO callers; either wire it into this feature or delete it.

items.title: GA4: Exit pages + attribution
items.notes: Advanced analytics. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant; the item is framed correctly. Build is additive GA4 runReport calls (exitPagePlusQueryString / first-user dimensions) plus surfacing. Note: it should plug into the analytics-intelligence bounded context (server/analytics-intelligence.ts) and ideally an intelligence slice if the data feeds AI context, per CLAUDE.md data-source wiring rules. P4 priority remains reasonable — this is advanced analytics, not core.

items.title: Webflow Enterprise API: 301 redirects
items.notes: Push rules via API (Enterprise-only). || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant but flag the Enterprise dependency for an owner decision: Webflow's redirect-rule WRITE API is Enterprise-only, so value is gated on whether any client is on Webflow Enterprise. Re-scope to 'add a write path that pushes scanner-recommended 301s to Webflow Enterprise redirect rules' built on top of the existing scan + recommendation output. Low priority (P4) is correct; build only when an Enterprise client materializes.

items.title: Sales report: email delivery
items.notes: Send reports directly to prospects. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant and genuinely cheap (the ~2h estimate is realistic) because server/email.ts:sendEmail + the existing HTML renderer already exist — this is just an endpoint + a prospect-email input in SalesReport.tsx. P4 is appropriate. Worth pairing with 89 if a lead-gen/outbound push is prioritized; otherwise leave parked.

items.title: Sales report: CRM integration
items.notes: Push prospect data to HubSpot/Pipedrive. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · OWNER DECISION · high conf]: Escalate to needs-owner-decision rather than auto-keep. This is a P4 outbound-sales/GTM bet: it only has value if the founder runs an outbound-prospecting motion that lives in HubSpot/Pipedrive. There is no evidence of that workflow in the platform (no CRM, no prospect pipeline, no lead model beyond ad-hoc sales reports). Recommend the owner confirm whether outbound-via-CRM is part of the business model; if not, mark obsolete-remove. If yes, it is a clean greenfield build dependent on which CRM is actually used.

items.title: Predictive SEO & trend forecasting
items.notes: Historical GSC data + Google Trends API + seasonal patterns → predict growing keywords 30-90 days out. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep, but re-scope and explicitly LINK to ov-seasonality-monthly-volumes — they share the same missing substrate (a per-keyword monthly series over time) and the same 'seasonal pattern' goal. Recommend MERGING the predictive layer with the seasonality item: build the durable monthly series + seasonal-event emitter first (seasonality item), then the 'predict growing keywords 30-90 days out' surface becomes a thin read on top. Note the FEATURE_VISION '8-12h' estimate is optimistic given the Google Trends integration and series-accrual (>=1yr) prerequisites. P4 priority is appropriate. || [MERGE 2026-06-24]: shares substrate with ov-seasonality-monthly-volumes (durable 12-month volume series). Build the monthly series + seasonal emitter THERE first; this item is then the thin "predict growing keywords" read on top — do not duplicate the series work.

items.title: Cross-client benchmarking network
items.notes: Anonymized benchmarks across all clients. Needs 20+ active clients. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · OWNER DECISION · medium conf]: Escalate to owner. This is a genuine founder judgment call on (a) whether a CLIENT-FACING cross-client benchmark is desirable/on-brand (vs keeping cross-client signal internal as it is today), and (b) whether the client base has reached the scale where it is meaningful and privacy-safe — the item's own '20+ active clients' gate is a business-scale decision, not a code state. sprints.name: 🧭 Platform Simplification — Complexity Audit (2026-06-24)
sprints.hours: Mixed S–L; prioritize P1 structural fixes first
sprints.rationale: Findings from the 22-agent double-focused platform audit (2026-06-24). Captures complexity/simplification work NOT handled as immediate quick-win code fixes. Every item is evidence-backed with file:line refs. Full report: the audit synthesis (complexity lens).
sprints.notes: Sequence: P1 structural correctness/scale fixes first (activity_log retention, recommendation_sets normalize, facade cycles + madge ratchet, CTR-curve reconcile, normalizePageUrl disambiguate, verify:* triage), then P2 decompositions/tuning, then P3 sweeps. Multi-node readiness (P4) is a gated checklist — do not act until a 2nd instance is on the roadmap. || [DELTA AUDIT 2026-06-24]: F1 (snapshot retention + serp index, mig 157), F2 (auto-send activity logging), and the reserved-flag lifecycle marker were FIXED in the delta-audit follow-up PR; the items above are the un-fixed delta findings.

items.title: Per-workspace activity_log retention (replace global 500-row cap)
items.notes: AUDIT high/small. server/activity-log.ts:166 MAX_ENTRIES=500 is GLOBAL; pruneOldest (lines 265-269) deletes oldest across ALL workspaces. Already pinned at 500 rows for 7 workspaces → at 70 workspaces each keeps ~7 entries, silently degrading client activity feeds + engagement signals. Fix: per-workspace cap or time cutoff (created_at < now-180d); move prune to a schedule, not synchronously on every insert (231 call sites hit a COUNT(*)+prune in a hot path). || DONE 2026-06-25: replaced the global activity_log cap with scheduled per-workspace retention (`MAX_ENTRIES_PER_WORKSPACE`) run through the existing daily data-retention cycle, so high-volume workspaces cannot evict quiet workspace feeds and `addActivity()` no longer runs count/prune work in the hot insert path. Added unit coverage proving quiet-workspace rows survive unrelated noisy-workspace writes and busy workspaces prune to 500 during the retention sweep.

items.title: Normalize recommendation_sets out of its JSON blob
items.notes: AUDIT high/med. recommendation_sets is "workspace_id PRIMARY KEY ... recommendations TEXT" — one row holds the whole array (avg 222KB, max 492KB, one workspace at 210 recs). updateRecommendationStatus() (recommendations.ts:415-436) parses+rewrites the ENTIRE blob for a single status flip; loadRecommendations is on warm intelligence/chat paths. Same anti-pattern migrations 088-090 fixed for keyword_gaps/topic_clusters. Fix: dedicated recommendations table (status/source/impact indexed); status updates become single-row UPDATEs. Add a pr-check rule for new large filterable JSON-array columns. || DONE 2026-06-25: added `recommendation_items` as the row-authoritative store while keeping `recommendation_sets` for generated_at/summary and legacy fallback. `loadRecommendations()` now reads normalized rows when present, falls back to the legacy blob when absent, and row-level status/lifecycle mutations update only the target row plus summary instead of rewriting the whole blob. Extracted public recommendation projection and route mutation helpers, added `docs/rules/recommendation-storage.md`, and added a pr-check ratchet (`data/json-array-column-baseline.json`) that blocks new unreviewed `TEXT DEFAULT '[]'` migration columns.

(b) Make slice-metadata-registry.ts slice refs lazy import thunks (it eagerly imports all 14 slices). (c) Add a madge --circular CI ratchet (madge already a devDependency, runs clean) baselined 109 server / 9 frontend, fail-on-increase; plus a routes→db/index import guard (only 6/93 route files touch the DB today). Re-export moved fns for one release; verify with madge. || DONE 2026-06-25: moved cache invalidation to `server/intelligence/cache-invalidation.ts`, shared cache state to `server/intelligence/cache-state.ts`, and persona formatting to `server/intelligence/persona-format.ts` while preserving facade re-exports. Post-refactor ratchet baseline is 92 server cycles / 10 src cycles; the direct route DB import guard documents the current 7 route exceptions and blocks new ones. Added leaf invalidation and source-contract coverage.

items.title: Disambiguate the two normalizePageUrl functions
items.notes: AUDIT high/small (silent page-identity mismatch). helpers.ts:161 re-exports the path-normalizer from shared/page-address-utils.ts:11; analytics-intelligence.ts:59 defines its OWN that preserves origin — and imports the shared one aliased as normalizePagePath, using both. Both feed dedup Map keys, so importing the wrong one silently produces mismatched page identities (~50 modules reference the name). Fix: rename the origin-preserving one to normalizePageUrlWithOrigin; drop the confusing local alias. || DONE 2026-06-25: renamed the analytics-only origin-preserving helper to `normalizePageUrlWithOrigin`, switched analytics path-only call sites back to the canonical `normalizePageUrl`, and updated unit coverage to make the distinction explicit.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — 🔑 Keyword Hub Health — First Paint, Memory, and Universe Read-Path Hardening
sprints.rationale: The Keyword Hub/Keyword Universe crash pattern was most likely not caused directly by circular imports; it was caused by heavy keyword/local/intelligence read paths sharing one hot graph under memory pressure. The current Hub is much safer than the original path: rows use a skinny page-bounded read path, local SEO refreshes run sequentially with heap headroom, rank evidence is capped, junk gates exist, and large local-candidate evaluation remains hard-capped. This sprint turns those patch-level protections into an explicit performance architecture: measure first, remove duplicated first-paint reads, move the remaining heavy local_candidates exception toward the skinny model, fix known facet drift, and add regression guardrails so a future universe expansion cannot quietly re-open the OOM/page-crash class.
sprints.notes: Audit anchors (2026-06-26): `src/components/KeywordHub.tsx` fetched `/summary` and `/rows` separately on first paint; `server/domains/keyword-command-center/summary-service.ts` and `rows-service.ts` duplicated source loading across strategy, page keywords, tracked keywords, latest ranks, feedback, lost visibility, and local visibility; `filter=local_candidates` dispatched through `buildKeywordCommandCenterRowsViaModel()` / `buildKeywordCommandCenterModel()` and required an explicit OOM cap; `summary-service.ts` hardcoded `notChecked: 0`; `candidate-boundary.ts` appeared to have a duplicated secondary-keyword junk check. DONE 2026-06-27: first paint uses the combined initial view, shared source snapshots back summary/rows/detail/initial, local_candidates uses a capped skinny projection instead of the full model, the dead Not Checked facet is no longer exposed, and the secondary junk check was re-audited as already gone. Success metric: Keyword Hub first paint remains stable on large workspaces while `npm run check:circular-deps`, focused KCC tests, and an explicit perf/heap smoke keep the read path inside known limits. Do not change keyword scoring semantics or lifecycle behavior in this sprint.

RequestList now uses the shared StatusBadge registry via a content-admin domain and removes cyan review-state drift, brief row quick actions are focus-within visible instead of hover-only, the Content Pipeline guide FAB is moved above bottom-right overlays, and content brief/request deletion is delayed with a visible Undo window before the server hard-delete commits. Added contract coverage for all four cleanup seams.

sprints.name: ✅ SHIPPED — 🧭 Platform Simplification — Parallel Explorer Recommendations
sprints.rationale: Six read-only parallel explorers audited the platform for behavior-preserving simplification opportunities: server domain shape, intelligence slices, frontend data flow, stacked feature surfaces, integration adapters, and test/guardrail leverage. This sprint captures those findings as a pick-up-ready backlog. The goal is not to remove functionality or pursue a broad rewrite; it is to make existing platform concepts more canonical by consolidating repeated lifecycle logic, cache invalidation, prompt assembly, provider transport, and guardrail patterns behind the conventions the codebase already uses.
sprints.notes: Source: 2026-06-09 parallel simplification explorer batch. All items are recommendation-only and pending. Before implementing any item: run the normal brainstorm/spec/pre-plan-audit/plan flow where the scope crosses multiple files; preserve behavior first; use phase-per-PR for high-blast-radius work; add parity tests before rewiring user-visible behavior. Recommended first PR: frontend invalidation helper extraction, starting with keyword mutation invalidation bundles before broader WS registry work.

items.title: Feature stack — consolidate client-facing insight projections into a read-only view model
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `src/components/client/Briefing/InsightsBriefingPage.tsx`, `src/components/client/InsightsEngine.tsx`, `src/components/client/AnalyticsTab.tsx`, `src/hooks/client/useClientBriefing.ts`, `src/hooks/client/useClientInsights.ts`, `src/hooks/client/useClientIntelligence.ts`, `src/hooks/useRecommendations.ts`, `server/routes/public-analytics.ts`, `server/routes/client-intelligence.ts`, `server/routes/recommendations.ts`, `server/briefing-client-projection.ts`, `server/insight-narrative.ts`, `server/monthly-digest.ts`. User-facing behavior to preserve: briefing magazine, monthly digest, recommendations, narrative insights, raw Analytics tab, Strategy deep links, and tier gating. Current complexity: client insight data is split across public intelligence, insight narrative, digest, briefing, recommendations, and raw GA4/GSC endpoints; components recompose overlapping summaries independently. Behavior-preserving path: introduce a read-only client insight view-model assembler returning named sections such as briefing, pulse, recommendations, narrative, digest, and intelligence summary. Keep existing endpoints during migration; raw Analytics charts stay separate. Tests/guardrails: client intelligence routes, briefing public tests, public analytics tests, hook/component coverage for briefing page, and public serialization tests proving admin-only fields remain scrubbed. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P2. Framing is still correct. Re-scope note: verify it stays behavior-preserving (keep existing endpoints + raw Analytics charts separate, per its own notes). This is a real-but-discretionary simplification, not a bug — sequence it after higher-impact client work, and pair it with the client IA redesign if/when that lands so the assembler matches the final tab shape. || PARTIAL 2026-06-26: extracted the `/api/public/intelligence/:workspaceId` tier slice selection and client-safe response projection into `server/client-insight-view-model.ts`; route now delegates as an HTTP adapter while preserving response shape, admin-only insight scrubbing, G3 pre-cap insight counts, tier-gated fields, keyword feedback sample caps, copy pipeline summary, and premium site-health/decay projections. Added pure view-model tests and a source-boundary contract. || PARTIAL 2026-06-26: moved client narrative insight projection ownership behind client insight view-model leaf modules, added client briefing and digest view entrypoints, rewired public narrative/digest and public/admin briefing preview routes to import through those leaf boundaries, and left compatibility exports for existing callers. || PARTIAL 2026-06-26: extracted public recommendation set/single-rec/response-summary projections into `server/client-insight-recommendation-view-model.ts`, keeping recommendation route mutations as HTTP orchestration while preserving public response shapes, The Issue flag gating, EMV stripping, operator wording/order projection, and response summary caps. Remaining scope: fold broader named dashboard sections into the shared read model without changing existing endpoints or raw Analytics charts. || DONE 2026-06-26: completed the client insight read-model consolidation by adding `src/hooks/client/useClientInsightViewModel.ts` as the client-side owner for narrative insights, monthly digest, published briefing, public intelligence, and public recommendation-set query contracts. Legacy hooks now delegate as compatibility wrappers, `InsightsEngine` reads recommendations through the shared section hook, and source-boundary tests prevent per-component query ownership from drifting back while raw Analytics charts remain separate as intended.

Server route growth should stop; reusable transitions move into content-domain services. Tests/guardrails: ContentBriefs component tests, ContentManager send-to-client tests, content brief/post/request route tests, status transition tests, and public post review broadcast tests. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · PARTIALLY DONE — re-scope to remaining delta · high conf]: Re-scope and keep, P2. items.title: Feature stack — centralize keyword opportunity display projection without merging scoring layers
items.source: Parallel simplification explorer audit 2026-06-09 — Feature Stack Simplification
items.notes: Scope: `server/scoring/keyword-value-score.ts`, `server/scoring/opportunity-value.ts`, `server/keyword-command-center.ts`, `server/keyword-strategy-enrichment.ts`, `server/keyword-recommendations.ts`, `server/briefing-client-projection.ts`, `src/components/client/strategy/StrategyKeywordDrawer.tsx`, `src/components/keyword-command-center/KeywordDetailDrawer.tsx`, `src/components/client/strategy/StrategyContentOpportunitiesSection.tsx`. User-facing behavior to preserve: Layer 1 Hub keyword value, Layer 2 recommendation opportunity value, client-safe explanations, content-gap ordering, and brief/request flows. Current complexity: scoring layers are intentionally separate, but display/ranking projections are scattered; surfaces consume `valueScore`, `opportunityScore`, fallback ordering, and 'why this matters' explanations differently. Behavior-preserving path: do not merge scoring algorithms. Add a shared projection helper for keyword-bearing opportunities that returns normalized keyword, display score, reasons, dollar value if allowed, client-safe explanation, and sort key. Use behind existing flags where ordering changes. Tests/guardrails: keyword value score tests, opportunity value tests, content gap scoring tests, keyword recommendation ranking tests, client strategy content gap tests, briefing preview tests, and flag-off parity tests. || [AUDIT 2026-06-24 · KEEP — still relevant · high conf]: Keep as still-relevant, P2. Framing is still correct — the 'do not merge scoring algorithms; add a shared display-projection helper returning normalized keyword + display score + reasons + client-safe explanation + sort key' approach is sound and the duplicate display helpers confirm the need. The duplicated `opportunityScore()` between pageIntelligenceDisplay.ts and the server scoring is the concrete consolidation target. || DONE 2026-06-26: Added `shared/keyword-opportunity-projection.ts` as the display/sort projection helper without importing or merging score-authority modules. Migrated briefing recommendations, strategy enrichment ordering, intelligence prompt content-gap ordering, client strategy keyword ordering, client content-gap backfilled ordering, and admin content-gap score/volume/priority ordering onto the shared helper. Added unit coverage for projection/comparator parity plus a source-contract guard preventing migrated surfaces from drifting back to local opportunity-score sort snippets.
